ogTitle: Lewis Ponds vs Leycester: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lewis Ponds and Leycester.
heading: Lewis Ponds vs Leycester — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lewis Ponds, New South Wales and Leycester, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lewis Ponds has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Leycester (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lewis Ponds or Leycester safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lewis Ponds has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Leycester scores 57/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lewis Ponds scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. Leycester scores 57/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lewis Ponds or Leycester?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lewis Ponds has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
